claim_chain: Ion channels are fundamental to cellular function and druggable but understudied
We computationally defined the full human channelome with rich functional annotation
We identified orthologs across 48,000 sequences spanning the tree of life
Conservation depth clusters ICs into functionally meaningful groups
We applied this to CALHM channels to identify conserved gating residues
Mutations in these conserved residues dramatically impair gating function
This provides groundwork for accelerating functional characterization of dark channels

typical_pattern: State what was accomplished for the first time
Describe the resource or method created
Connect to broader implications or future directions

likely_priorities: Validation of computational predictions with experimental data
Accuracy and reliability of LLM-based literature mining
Completeness and correctness of orthology assignments
Functional relevance of identified conserved residues
Generalizability of findings beyond CALHM channels
likely_attacks: LLM limitations in entity recognition and nomenclature handling
High frequency of evidence-not-found responses in RAG system
Potential overcorrections or misinterpretations without adequate validation
Limited set of homologs in prior studies versus comprehensive tree-of-life analysis
Exception cases like W113 in CALHM6 that did not impair function
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly discusses limitations of the RAG system including 'diverse data presentation formats, complex channel subunit relationships, and variant gene nomenclature, which hinder its effectiveness' and 'high frequency of responses where evidence was not found'. It acknowledges concerns about 'potential overcorrections or misinterpretations without adequate validation'. The authors defend their approach by contrasting with 'prior studies examined conserved residues in CALHM channels' based on 'a limited set of homologs from model organisms' versus their 'thousands of CALHM sequences collected from the entire tree of life'. They also note the exception 'mutations in an analogous position W113 in CALHM6 did not impair channel function, presenting an exception compared to other residues'.

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based entirely on the provided discussion, abstract, and figure legend content.
